WFRP4EPARTY.NoMembers: No members yet. Drag a character onto this sheet to add them.
WFRP4EPARTY.RemoveMemberBody: Remove this character from the party?
WFRP4EPARTY.DropRejected: Only player characters can be added to a party.
WFRP4EPARTY.NoCombat: A party actor cannot be added to combat.
WFRP4EPARTY.InventoryPlaceholder: Shared inventory is coming in a later phase.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyPlaceholder: The journey log is coming in a later phase.
WFRP4EPARTY.SummaryLowestMoveHint: Slowest member's Move — drives the party's journey Stage count
WFRP4EPARTY.GMOnly: Only the GM may run group tests.
WFRP4EPARTY.OwnerOnly: Only the GM or this character's owner may do that.
WFRP4EPARTY.GroupTestPicker: Roll a custom skill or characteristic for every party member
WFRP4EPARTY.AllowAdvancedFallback: Allow characteristic fallback for members without this skill
WFRP4EPARTY.InventoryEmpty: The party pool is empty. Drag an item here to deposit it.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferFailed: The transfer could not be completed.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferItemMissing: That item is no longer available to transfer.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferBadAmount: Enter a valid amount to transfer.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferInsufficientFunds: Not enough coin in the source to cover that transfer.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferCreateFailed: Could not add {item} to the target. Nothing was moved.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferVerifyFailed: Could not confirm {item} arrived safely. Nothing was moved.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferSourceFailed: Could not remove {item} from the source. Rolling back.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferMemberGone: That party member is no longer available.
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferRollbackFailed: Transfer rollback failed for {actor} ({item}). Check both sheets manually.
WFRP4EPARTY.RecuperateMarkerTooltip: Recuperating — will heal an extra Toughness Bonus Wounds on next Rest
WFRP4EPARTY.RecuperateConfirmBody: Confirm this member has not suffered a Fatigued Condition during this Stage.
WFRP4EPARTY.RecuperateBonusLine: {name} was taking it easy and heals an extra {tb} Wound(s).
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampTooltip: Each camper rolls a hidden Challenging (+0) camp test (their better of Outdoor Survival / Heal); the SL pool removes Fatigued or heals Wounds across the party
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampHint: Each camper rolls their better camp skill (shown below) at Challenging (+0). All SL pool together; a failed test gives that camper a Fatigued Condition.
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampNoCampers: Pick at least one member to make camp.
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampAllocateHint: 1 SL removes one Fatigued Condition or heals one Wound — on any member. Adjust the suggestion and apply.
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampOverspent: That allocation spends {spent} SL but the pool only has {pool}.
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampVerifyFailed: Could not confirm the camp result was applied to {name}. Check their sheet manually.
WFRP4EPARTY.MakeCampAlreadyResolvedThisS: Make Camp has already been resolved for this Stage — nothing more to roll. Advance to a new Stage to camp again.
WFRP4EPARTY.RestPartyTooltip: Every member makes a hidden Average (+20) Endurance Test; the healing (SL + Toughness Bonus, plus the Recuperate bonus) is applied automatically
WFRP4EPARTY.RestVerifyFailed: Could not confirm {name}'s healing was applied. Check their sheet manually.
WFRP4EPARTY.ExposureRuleSettingHint: When a Journey Stage's weather qualifies (Rain/Snow/Downpour/Blizzard without shelter), show an Exposure reminder and a hidden group Endurance test button. Consequences stay GM-narrated — no automation.
WFRP4EPARTY.UseFallbackTablesSetting: Include Homebrew Tables in Encounter Draws (Journey)
WFRP4EPARTY.UseFallbackTablesSettingHint: When wfrp4e-eis is active, encounter draws merge EiS's own results with this module's homebrew fallback tables into one combined pool. Uncheck to draw from EiS's tables only. Simple mode (no EiS) always uses the homebrew tables regardless of this setting.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneySimpleModeNotice: Simple mode - Enemy in Shadows (wfrp4e-eis) module is not active or not owned. To unlock the full engine, purchase and activate the module.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyDestinationTooltip: The journey's destination — a free-text label for the GM's reference, purely descriptive.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyTotalStagesTooltip: How many Stages this journey has. RAW modifiers (party Move, mounts, a successful Navigation/Lore Test) only ever ADVISE this number — the GM sets it directly.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneySeasonTooltip: The current in-world season — drives which seasonal weather table is drawn from when Rolling Weather.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyStatusTooltip: Whether the party is currently travelling, and which Stage they're on.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndJourneyBody: End this journey and return to the idle state? The log is kept; a new journey can be started afterward.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyHintLowMove: Slowest member's Move is 3 or less — RAW suggests +1–2 Stages.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyHintMounted: The whole party is mounted at Move 6+ — RAW suggests halving Stages (minimum 1).
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyHintNavigation: A Challenging (+0) Navigation or Lore (relevant) Test — Average (+20) with a proper map — can reduce Stages by 1 (minimum 1) on success.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyNavigationReductionSu: Success — advisory: the GM may reduce the Stage count by 1 (minimum 1).
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyRollWeatherTooltip: Draw this Stage's weather from the season's Enemy in Shadows table, apply any carried modifier, and whisper the result + effects to the GM.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEisTableMissing: The wfrp4e-eis table could not be found.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyExposureReminder: Exposure rule is active — this weather may call for the Exposure Test (button below, next to Make Camp / Rest & Recover).
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyExposureConsequenceNo: The general Exposure effect ("remains until warmed at shelter") stays GM-narrated. Automated: Common Cold contraction in Winter/Spring only.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyExposureTestStripTool: Roll the Challenging (+0) Endurance Exposure Test for the whole party in one batch. In Winter/Spring, a failed test automatically contracts the Common Cold (embeds the disease Item — its own symptoms, e.g. Malaise's Fatigued, apply via the system as normal). Gear penalties (Downpour/Blizzard missing coat/tent) are not tracked — apply manually if relevant.
WFRP4EPARTY.PickExposureMembersHint: All party members are pre-checked. Uncheck anyone exempt (e.g. has both a good coat and a tent/shelter) before rolling.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyCustomTableMissing: The custom table set for this category could not be found.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyFallbackTableMissing: The module's fallback table could not be found — has the world been relaunched since the pack was declared?
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyCustomTablesHint: Point a category's Draw button at any world or compendium RollTable UUID instead of the built-in EiS+fallback pool. Leave blank to use the default pool.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEndeavourHint: Pick one Endeavour per member for this Stage (Assign), optionally dial in a manual Modifier (e.g. a narrative encounter bonus), then click Resolve Endeavours to roll everything at once.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyAssignEndeavourToolti: Which Endeavour this member spends this Stage on. Rolls (if any) happen when you click Resolve Endeavours below, not immediately.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEndeavourModifierTool: Manual roll modifier for this Endeavour (e.g. a narrative encounter bonus like "+10 to next Forage") — dial it in before Resolve Endeavours; nothing applies automatically (D15).
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyResolveEndeavoursTool: Roll every assigned-but-unresolved Endeavour for this Stage as one GM-whispered batch. This can take a moment — results and any Fatigued confirm appear after.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyNoUnresolvedEndeavour: No unresolved Endeavours to roll — assign one first.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEndeavoursResolvedCou: Resolved {count} Endeavour(s) — see the GM-whispered chat message for results.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyFailFatiguedBody: The following members failed and gain a Fatigued Condition:
WFRP4EPARTY.RecuperateBlockedFatigued: This member gained Fatigued this Stage and cannot Recuperate.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourForageDesc: Challenging (+0) Outdoor Survival Test — search the surrounding land for food.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourGatherInformationDe: Challenging (+0) Gossip Test — ask the GM up to your SL questions about local matters.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourKeepWatchDesc: Challenging (+0) Perception Test — on any success, the whole party cannot be Surprised this Stage.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourPracticeASkillDesc: Challenging (+0) Test with a chosen skill — success lets you reverse the roll on one future Test with that skill.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourWoodcraftDesc: Challenging (+0) Outdoor Survival Test, harder the worse the weather — success waives the Exposure Test for the whole party this Stage.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourMapTheRouteDesc: Extended Trade (Cartography) or Art (Drawing) Test toward a cumulative SL target — a finished map can later reduce Stages.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourMakeCampDesc: Challenging (+0) Outdoor Survival or Heal Test — the SL pool clears Fatigued or heals Wounds across everyone who made camp.
WFRP4EPARTY.EndeavourRecuperateDesc: No roll — counts this Stage as taking it easy. Blocked if you gained Fatigued this Stage.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyKeepWatchFlag: No Surprise this Stage (Keep Watch succeeded)
WFRP4EPARTY.ResetLogTooltip: Clear the journey log. Does not affect Stage state, config, or anything else — only the log entries.
WFRP4EPARTY.ResetLogConfirmBody: Clear every entry in the journey log? This cannot be undone.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyArrivalDiseaseReminde: Reminder: incubating diseases become full-blown outbreaks during Arrival (informational — no automation).
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyNoLoreSkills: No party member owns a Lore (region) skill.
WFRP4EPARTY.WeatherEffectRain: Visibility reduced to 75ft; −10 to ranged Tests.
WFRP4EPARTY.WeatherEffectDownpour: −10 to all physical Tests; −20 to ranged Tests; gunpowder weapons are ruined.
WFRP4EPARTY.WeatherEffectSnow: Average (+20) Endurance Test required or gain Fatigued; movement may be hindered.
WFRP4EPARTY.WeatherEffectBlizzard: Challenging (+0) Endurance Test required or gain Fatigued; visibility and movement severely hindered.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEndeavourSkillMissing: Choose a skill for the assigned Endeavour before resolving: {names}. Those assignments were left unresolved.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyEndeavourAlreadyResol: This Endeavour has already been resolved this Stage.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyMemberNotInParty: That actor is not a current member of this party.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyInvalidEndeavour: That is not a valid journey Endeavour.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyInvalidSkillChoice: That skill is not available for this Endeavour.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyMutationFailed: The journey changed before that action could be applied. Refresh the sheet and try again.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyStageBusy: The GM is resolving this Stage. Try that journey change again when resolution finishes.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneySuggestedTooltip: Suggested by this Stage's Endeavour outcomes (hint only — nothing is drawn automatically)
WFRP4EPARTY.StartJourneyConfirmBody: Starting a new journey clears the previous journey's Stage records and log. Continue?
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyArrivalNoPenalties: No one arrives Fatigued — no first-impression Fellowship penalties.
WFRP4EPARTY.JourneyMapTheRouteNoSkill: Map the Route requires Trade (Cartography) or Art (Drawing).
WFRP4EPARTY.TransferCapacityExceeded: Not enough capacity — this would push the pool {shortfall} Enc over its limit.
WFRP4EPARTY.DeleteItemConfirmBody: Remove this item from the party pool? This cannot be undone.
WFRP4EPARTY.VehicleConnectedNotification: {name} connected to the party as its vehicle.
WFRP4EPARTY.VehicleDisconnectBody: Disconnect this vehicle from the party? Its capacity contribution will be removed.
WFRP4EPARTY.QuestItemProtected: That is a quest item — only the GM may move or alter it.
WFRP4EPARTY.QuestItemsEmpty: No quest items. Drag an item here to mark it as one.
WFRP4EPARTY.PoolMoveFailed: Could not remove that item from the party pool — the drop was undone.
WFRP4EPARTY.PoolMoveDuplicate: "{name}" may now exist in BOTH the party pool and the member's inventory — please check and delete one.
WFRP4EPARTY.RevealStatsTooltip: Show this member's stats to players. Off by default for NPCs, on by default for player characters.
WFRP4EPARTY.CollapseMemberTooltip: Collapse — show only this member's portrait and name. Affects your view only.
WFRP4EPARTY.ExpandMemberTooltip: Expand — show this member's full stats again.
WFRP4EPARTY.CapacitySettingsHint: Controls how much the party pool can carry: 1 Enc per member (PC or NPC), plus a connected vehicle's Carries value, plus this flat Bonus.
WFRP4EPARTY.CapacityBonusHint: Flat Encumbrance added to the pool's capacity — use this for saddlebags, a hired porter, or any other narrative capacity boost that isn't a vehicle.
WFRP4EPARTY.VehicleHint: Drag a Vehicle actor from the sidebar or a compendium onto this sheet to connect it — its Carries value is added to the pool's capacity. Connect as many vehicles as you like; use the X next to a vehicle to disconnect it.
WFRP4EPARTY.ConsolidateDisabledInvalidCh: Consolidate is disabled — the configured currencies do not divide evenly into each other, so an automatic minimal redistribution cannot be guaranteed correct.
WFRP4EPARTY.CurrencyChainInvalid: This world's currencies ({larger}/{smaller}) do not form a clean divisibility chain — Consolidate is disabled until the Item Piles currency config is fixed. Deposit and withdraw still work normally.
WFRP4EPARTY.MemberCategoryTooltip: Tag this member as a Companion or Henchman — moves them out of the PC/NPC list into their own section
WFRP4EPARTY.InventorySortAlphaTooltip: Sort the pool alphabetically (personal preference — never changes the GM's manual order for other viewers)
